report_name: New 'Gentlemen' Ransomware Uses EDR Killer Framework to Blindside Security Tools

threat_actors.name: The Gentlemen
threat_actors.description: A new Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) group that deploys a suite of EDR and AV termination tools, the GentleKiller EDR Framework, as a preliminary step before initiating its encryption routine.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware deployment after disabling endpoint security.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: The GentleKiller EDR Framework was used to disable a wide array of EDR and antivirus products by stopping services, killing processes, and unloading kernel drivers before encryption.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Gentlemen ransomware encrypts files on local drives, connected removable media, and accessible network shares after disabling security defenses.

attack_patterns.name: Modify Registry
attack_patterns.description: The GentleKiller EDR Framework modifies registry keys to disable security features as part of its defense evasion strategy.

attack_patterns.name: Service Stop
attack_patterns.description: The GentleKiller EDR Framework attempts to stop security-related services using commands like 'sc stop <service_name>' to neutralize endpoint defenses.

malware.name: Gentlemen Ransomware
malware.description: A ransomware variant that first deploys the GentleKiller EDR Framework to disable security tools before encrypting files on local and network drives.

malware.name: GentleKiller EDR Framework
malware.description: A collection of known and custom scripts and binaries designed to terminate EDR and AV products by stopping services, killing processes, unloading kernel drivers, and modifying registry keys.

tools.name: PC Hunter
tools.description: A legitimate system utility whose driver (PCHunter.sys) is abused by the GentleKiller EDR Framework to terminate protected security processes.

tools.name: Process Explorer
tools.description: A legitimate system utility from Sysinternals whose driver (PROCEXP.sys) is abused by the GentleKiller EDR Framework to terminate protected security processes.

identities.name: Amigest
identities.description: An organization targeted in a successful attack by The Gentlemen ransomware.

identities.name: Burris & MacOmber, PLL
identities.description: A law firm targeted in a successful attack by The Gentlemen ransomware.

identities.name: COFAQ
identities.description: An organization targeted in a successful attack by The Gentlemen ransomware.

identities.name: CrowdStrike
identities.description: An EDR vendor whose products are targeted for termination by the GentleKiller EDR Framework.

identities.name: SentinelOne
identities.description: An EDR vendor whose products are targeted for termination by the GentleKiller EDR Framework.

identities.name: Sophos
identities.description: An EDR/AV vendor whose products are targeted for termination by the GentleKiller EDR Framework.

indicators.name: Abused EDR Killer Tool Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Execution of Abused EDR Killer Tool
id: 2b8c1f3a-9e4d-4f8b-8c1d-9a3b2c1d0e9f
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate tools like Process Explorer or PC Hunter which are known to be abused by EDR killer frameworks like GentleKiller.
references:
  - https://threat-modeling.com/vulnerability-intelligence-report-june-19-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1562.001
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\procexp.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution or presence of tools known to be part of the GentleKiller EDR Framework.

indicators.name: EDR/AV Service Stop Command
indicators.pattern:
title: EDR/AV Service Stop Attempt
id: 5d9e8f7a-6b4c-4d3e-9f1a-2b3c4d5e6f7a
status: experimental
description: Detects command-line attempts to stop common EDR or AV services, a common precursor to ransomware deployment as seen with Gentlemen Ransomware.
references:
  - https://threat-modeling.com/vulnerability-intelligence-report-june-19-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1489
  - attack.t1562.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_sc:
    Image|endswith: '\sc.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' stop '
  selection_net:
    Image|endswith: '\net.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' stop '
  keywords:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'CsbAgent'
      - 'SentinelAgent'
      - 'Sophos'
      - 'MsSense'
      - 'SAVService'
      - 'wscsvc'
  condition: (selection_sc or selection_net) and keywords
indicators.description: Detects command-line attempts to stop common EDR or AV services.

indicators.name: EDR/AV Process Kill Command
indicators.pattern:
title: EDR/AV Process Kill Attempt
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects command-line attempts to terminate common EDR or AV processes using taskkill, a technique used by Gentlemen Ransomware's EDR killer framework.
references:
  - https://threat-modeling.com/vulnerability-intelligence-report-june-19-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1562.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\taskkill.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '/IM '
      - '/F'
  keywords:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'csfalcon.exe'
      - 'SentinelOne.exe'
      - 'Sophos.exe'
      - 'MsMpEng.exe'
  condition: selection and keywords
indicators.description: Detects command-line attempts to forcefully terminate EDR/AV processes.

indicators.name: Abused EDR Killer Driver Load
indicators.pattern:
title: Loading of Abused EDR Killer Driver
id: 9f8e7d6c-5b4a-4c3d-b2a1-9f8e7d6c5b4a
status: experimental
description: Detects the loading of legitimate but frequently abused drivers used to terminate protected processes, as seen with the GentleKiller framework.
references:
  - https://threat-modeling.com/vulnerability-intelligence-report-june-19-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1562.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ImageLoaded|endswith:
      - '\PCHunter.sys'
      - '\PROCEXP.sys'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the loading of drivers known to be abused by EDR killer tools.
